ontology.foodex2_description: Rice wine (fermented alcoholic beverage from rice)
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for rice wine as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for fermented grain beverages is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of rice wine is approximately 30 g (one small cup or shot glass), as it is consumed in small quantities due to its alcoholic content.

nutrient_source.title: Rice wine, nutritional composition per 100g — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for rice wine (generic). Energy 105 kcal, carbohydrate 26.25 g, protein 0 g, fat 0 g, sodium 2 mg confirmed from HPB record D01050003.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for generic rice wine exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 89 (high) based on the analogous fermented rice beverage profile: the carbohydrate fraction (26.25 g/100 g) consists predominantly of residual glucose and maltose from rice starch hydrolysis, which are rapidly absorbed. This is consistent with the GI range reported for glucose-dominant fermented grain beverages (GI 85–95) in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per 30 g serving = (89 × 26.25 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 7.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content — directly raises blood pressure and interacts with antihypertensive medications
hypertension.guidance: Rice wine is an alcoholic beverage and is contraindicated for individuals managing hypertension; even moderate alcohol consumption acutely elevates systolic and diastolic blood pressure and reduces the efficacy of antihypertensive medications.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (26.25 g/100 g) combined with alcohol-mediated impairment of hepatic glucose regulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rice wine is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high carbohydrate content (26.25 g/100 g, GI ~89), which imposes a significant glycaemic burden, and because alcohol impairs hepatic glucose output and can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms in patients on insulin or sulfonylureas.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol consumption increases cardiovascular risk through elevated blood pressure, arrhythmia risk, and cardiomyopathy with regular use
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Rice wine should be avoided by individuals with cardiovascular disease, as regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure, increases triglyceride levels, and is associated with increased risk of atrial fibrillation and alcoholic cardiomyopathy.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is pro-inflammatory and critically contraindicated with methotrexate and biologic DMARDs used in RA management
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Rice wine is contraindicated for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis on disease-modifying therapy; alcohol is directly pro-inflammatory and even small amounts significantly increase hepatotoxicity risk when combined with methotrexate or biologic agents.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rice wine is a traditional Asian alcoholic beverage produced by fermenting cooked rice using yeast and koji mold (Aspergillus oryzae), which converts rice starch into fermentable sugars and then into alcohol. It is consumed across East and Southeast Asia in various forms including Chinese mijiu, Japanese sake, Korean makgeolli, and Vietnamese ruou gao. The beverage is typically clear to pale yellow, with a mildly sweet, slightly tangy flavour and a smooth, light-bodied texture. Alcohol content varies widely by style, typically ranging from 6% to 20% ABV. Nutritionally, rice wine is notable for its high carbohydrate content (26.25 g/100 g) derived from residual rice sugars, negligible protein and fat, and low sodium (2 mg/100 g). The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 89 (high), making it a high-glycaemic beverage. Clinically, rice wine is contraindicated for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Alcohol impairs hepatic glucose regulation, raises blood pressure, increases cardiovascular risk, and interacts dangerously with common medications including methotrexate, sulfonylureas, and antihypertensives. Despite its cultural significance as a cooking ingredient and social beverage across Asia, it should be consumed with caution or avoided entirely in clinical dietary contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in rice wine?
What is rice wine made from?
Is rice wine the same as sake?
Is rice wine suitable for hypertension?
What is the carbohydrate content of rice wine?
Can I drink rice wine if I have rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (record D01050003) with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) due to absence of a direct rice wine GI measurement in the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB source D01050003; rice wine is a fermented beverage with negligible protein content — confirmed zero. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB source D01050003; rice wine contains no fat by composition — confirmed zero. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero as fat_g is zero. sugar_g is reported as 0.0 per HPB; residual sugars are captured within the total carbohydrate figure of 26.25 g/100 g. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid fermented beverage with no fibre-containing solids. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for generic rice wine not available in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central at the level of this generic record as of 2026. GI value of 89 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for generic rice wine was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Estimate based on glucose-dominant fermented grain beverage profile per Atkinson et al. 2008. Ontology: FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A00LN used as best defensible matches for rice wine as a fermented alcoholic grain beverage; codes should be verified against current ontology releases. SNOMED CT code not assigned due to limited food-item coverage in SNOMED CT for fermented grain beverages.
